seo.title: OpenAI Assistants API Guardrails for Healthcare
seo.description: Compliance guide for OpenAI Assistants API agents in healthcare. Meet HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Healthcare teams deploying OpenAI Assistants API face immense risks due to the handling of Protected Health Information (PHI) through code interpreters, file search, and function calling. Without compliance guardrails, Assistants can inadvertently expose PHI, execute unsafe operations on patient data, or hallucinate invalid medical decisions, violating HIPAA and FDA regulations. Vex provides verifiable guardrails that ensure patient data security, auditability, and regulatory compliance in every Assistant interaction.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires PHI to remain secure, de-identified before processing, and access strictly controlled with audit trails.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies PHI de-identification via PII detection policies and enforces data residency with runtime assertions before LLM processing.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates electronic records be trustworthy, auditable, and protected against unauthorized changes.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex creates immutable audit logs of all Assistant runs, verifying code interpreter outputs and function call parameters against medical schemas.

complianceRequirements.description: Imposes breach notification requirements and strengthens HIPAA enforcement for PHI handling.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex detects PHI exposure attempts in real-time via webhook verification, preventing breaches and generating compliance reports.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires demonstrated controls for security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides continuous verification of Assistant behavior against SOC 2 policies, ensuring thread context limits and secure file retrieval.

industryRisks.name: PHI Exposure via Code Interpreter
industryRisks.description: Sandbox executes patient data operations that leak PHI to external APIs or generate unencrypted files.
industryRisks.example: Assistant processes MRI scan data and uploads results to unsecured cloud storage, violating HIPAA.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated Function Calls
industryRisks.description: Assistant generates invalid medical dosage arguments outside validated schemas.
industryRisks.example: Function call hallucinates 'dosage: 500mg' for pediatric patient when schema limits to 50mg max.

industryRisks.name: Uncontrolled Thread Context Growth
industryRisks.description: Conversation accumulates PHI beyond token limits, causing silent data truncation or exposure.
industryRisks.example: Patient history thread exceeds 128k tokens, dropping critical allergy information mid-conversation.

industryRisks.name: Irrelevant PHI File Retrieval
industryRisks.description: File search returns outdated or unauthorized patient records during diagnosis.
industryRisks.example: Assistant retrieves 2-year-old chart for current patient, leading to incorrect treatment recommendation.

implementation.title: Webhook Verification on Run Completion
implementation.description: Add Vex verification to OpenAI run completion events to check for PHI exposure and schema compliance.

implementation.title: TypeScript Webhook Handler
implementation.description: Handle OpenAI webhook events with Vex verification for function calling schema compliance.

implementation.title: PHI De-identification Preprocessing
implementation.description: Verify patient data is de-identified before Assistant processing using Vex policies.
implementation.code:
from openai import OpenAI
from vex_sdk import VexClient

openai_client = OpenAI()
vex_client = VexClient(api_key='your-vex-key')

def create_safe_assistant_thread(patient_input: str):
    # Pre-verify PHI de-identification
    verification = vex_client.verify({
        'text': patient_input,
        'policy': 'phi-deidentification-healthcare',
        'threshold': 0.01  # <1% PHI detection
    })
    
    if not verification.passed:
        raise ValueError('PHI detected - de-identify before processing')
    
    # Safe Assistant call
    thread = openai_client.beta.threads.create()
    openai_client.beta.threads.messages.create(
        thread_id=thread.id,
        role='user',
        content=patient_input
    )
    
    run = openai_client.beta.threads.runs.create(
        thread_id=thread.id,
        assistant_id='asst-healthcare-compliant'
    )
    
    return {'thread_id': thread.id, 'run_id': run.id, 'verified': True}

caseStudyScenario.heading: OpenAI Assistants API in Healthcare: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Dr. Sarah's telemedicine practice deployed OpenAI Assistants API to assist with patient triage and medication recommendations. The Assistant was configured with code interpreter for dosage calculations, file search across EHR systems, and function calling to pharmacy APIs. During a busy flu season, the system processed 500+ patient interactions daily, pulling historical records and generating treatment plans.

Without guardrails, the Assistant began hallucinating function call parameters, recommending adult dosages for pediatric patients. File search retrieved outdated allergy records, and thread contexts grew beyond 128k tokens, silently truncating critical PHI. A code interpreter sandbox session inadvertently wrote patient data to an unsecured temp file, exposing 47 patients' records.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex guardrails were deployed as run completion webhooks, verifying every Assistant output against HIPAA/FDA policies. PHI detection blocked 98% of unde-identified inputs, schema validation caught dosage hallucinations, and context limits triggered thread resets. The practice achieved SOC 2 Type II compliance, eliminated PHI breaches, and reduced malpractice risk by 100%. Regulatory audits confirmed full auditability of 250k+ interactions.
